title: Lawson Students Attend Women in Law Enforcement Conference
excerpt: Lawson criminal justice students connected with leaders in policing at the annual Women in Law Enforcement Conference hosted by the Jefferson County Sheriff's Office.
image_alt: Lawson Students Attend Women in Law Enforcement Conference
paragraphs: For students interested in law enforcement and legal careers, getting to know people in those professions is huge, and for several Lawson students, that was a key takeaway from attending the recent 3rd Annual Women in Law Enforcement conference, hosted by the Jefferson County Sheriff's Office.

Several students joined dozens of area women in law enforcement who came to the event, which was themed "She. Leads. She Serves. She Protects." They accompanied Dr. Alana N. Williams, a criminal justice instructor at the college.

"The experience showed us that women in law enforcement are not only present, but powerful, influential, and shaping the future of the profession," said Dr. Williams. "The students gained exposure to women doing the work, making their career goals feel more attainable and tangible."

Participating students included Jemerial Woodard, Sharica McCombs, and Kaliyah Reese-Stokes. They heard from women leaders in a variety of local enforcement roles, met Jefferson County Sheriff Mark Pettway, and had a chance to see former Birmingham Police Chief Annette Nunn, who in 2003 became the first woman to lead the local police department.
Also present was Congresswoman Terri Sewell (D-AL). She formally introduced keynote speaker, former U.S. Rep. Val Demings, who served as Orlando's police chief before going to Washington.
Her challenge to those who attended the conference?

"Nothing less than your full potential is acceptabl...we are living in some strange times. We are living in some critical moments. Half of your service just isn't good enough," she said. "We need to remove all the excuses, the barriers, and how we feel about ourselves so we can be our absolute best. Meeting this moment demands nothing less than our best service and our best commitment."
The former congresswoman encouraged the audience to focus on their strengths and to be less concerned with naysayers.

"It's not so much what other people think, because there will always be those who try to make you think you are in the wrong place at the wrong time," she said. "They don't matter. What really matters is what they make you think about yourself and your ability to do the job that you're in."

title: Local Zeta Phi Beta Chapter Announces Scholarship Opportunity
excerpt: Zeta Phi Beta Sorority, Inc. Announces Scholarship for Lawson State and Local High School Students
image_alt: 2 women giving scholarship check to Lawson Student
paragraphs: Zeta Phi Beta Sorority, Inc., Lambda Zeta Zeta Chapter (Bessemer, Alabama), is now accepting applications for two 2026 scholarship opportunities designed to support academic achievement and community engagement among young women. Both scholarships carry a March 15 submission deadline and aim to uplift students who demonstrate academic promise, leadership, and service.
Scholarship Application for Current Lawson State Community College Students

Lambda Zeta Zeta Chapter is offering a competitive scholarship of up to $500 for a current female student enrolled at Lawson State Community College. Applicants must have a minimum 2.5 GPA, at least one semester remaining, and must submit an official transcript, a written essay, proof of community service, and a letter of recommendation from a community representative.
Applications must be mailed or submitted through the chapter's preferred portal and received no later than March 15.
Scholarship Application for Graduating High School Seniors

A second opportunity is available for graduating female high school seniors who plan to pursue higher education at a community college or four-year institution. Eligible students must have at least a 2.5 GPA, submit an official high school transcript, provide proof of community service, and include two letters of recommendation - one academic and one service-related.

The college is proud to partner with supportive organizations across the region as they invest their time, money and resources in Lawson State students. For more information about scholarships and other forms of financial aid, email finaid@lawsonstate.edu.
